_comment: Canonical YouTube algorithm signal data. Structured for use by seo-intelligence-engine. NOT keyword data — this is algorithm weight and best-practice data. Updated when source-registry sources (youtube-help-recommendations, youtube-creator-blog, youtube-help-analytics) detect changes. Source IDs are back-references to source-registry.json.

algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: Design thumbnail and title together as a unit. Thumbnail drives the first glance; title confirms the premise. For a new channel in a competitive niche, above 5% CTR on home/browse is strong. Test thumbnails by publishing, waiting 48 hours, and swapping if CTR is below 3%.
algorithm_signals.trap_to_avoid: Clickbait that does not match the video content. YouTube surveys viewers post-watch; a clickbait mismatch depresses the satisfaction score and reduces future recommendation.
algorithm_signals.freshness_note: Current as of mid-2026. YouTube reweights signals without public notice — verify source monthly.

algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: Maximize total minutes watched per video, not just completion percentage. A 20-minute video watched 50% (10 minutes) earns more signal than a 5-minute video watched 100% (5 minutes). Do not artificially shorten videos to boost completion rate at the cost of watch time.
algorithm_signals.trap_to_avoid: Padding video length with filler to increase watch time. YouTube's satisfaction signal (post-watch survey) will decline, net-negating the watch time gain.

algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: The shape of the retention curve matters more than the final percentage. A flat line at 40% is a stronger signal than a line that drops from 80% to 20%. Hook within the first 30 seconds. Add re-engagement moments every 3 to 5 minutes to flatten the slope.
algorithm_signals.trap_to_avoid: A sharp retention drop at a specific timestamp is a solvable problem. Identify what is happening at that timestamp in the video and address it in future content.

algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: Publish when the audience is most active. Check YouTube Studio audience tab for the creator's specific audience activity pattern. Encourage early engagement in the first 60 seconds of the video: tell viewers what to do (comment below, save this video) explicitly and specifically.
algorithm_signals.trap_to_avoid: Generic CTAs ('like and subscribe if you enjoyed this'). Specific CTAs ('comment your biggest thrift store challenge below') drive higher engagement rate.

algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: High-CTR thumbnails are more likely to be session starters because they are surfaced to users not already in a YouTube session. A new channel can disproportionately benefit from this signal by investing heavily in thumbnail quality.
algorithm_signals.trap_to_avoid: Underinvesting in thumbnail design when the channel is new. Session starts compound — a video that starts sessions continues to receive home feed distribution weeks after publishing.

algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: End screen content should be contextually relevant to the video that just ended. A viewer who just watched a fall mantel video is most likely to click a related fall decor video or a 'how I thrifted everything on this mantel' follow-up. Match the end screen video to the viewer's demonstrated interest.
algorithm_signals.trap_to_avoid: Defaulting to the most recent upload as the end screen video regardless of topic relevance.

shorts_signals.current_best_practice: A Short that gets replayed signals strong entertainment or utility value. Design Shorts with a loop point: the last frame should flow naturally back to the first frame so a replay feels intentional. Completion rate is the dominant signal for Shorts distribution.
A viewer who swipes away immediately signals low relevance. The first frame is the thumbnail for Shorts. Text overlay and strong visual in the first frame reduce immediate swipe-through.

ctr_benchmarks._note: All benchmarks labeled [estimated, unverified]. Verify against the creator's actual YouTube Studio data before making recommendations. These are niche-wide starting points only.
